easy.prompt: How do you return all columns from table students?
answers: SELECT * FROM students;
GET ALL FROM students;
SELECT students ALL;
SHOW students;

easy.prompt: Which query returns only the name column from employees?
answers: SELECT name employees;
SELECT name FROM employees;
GET name FROM employees;
SELECT COLUMN(name) employees;

easy.prompt: How do you filter customers from Cairo?
answers: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE city = 'Cairo';
SELECT * FROM customers IF city = 'Cairo';
GET customers WHERE city = 'Cairo';
SELECT city = 'Cairo' FROM customers;

easy.prompt: Which query sorts products by price from low to high?
answers: SELECT * FROM products SORT BY price;
SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
SELECT ASC price FROM products;
ORDER products BY price;

easy.prompt: How do you show only 5 rows from books in SQLite?
answers: SELECT * FROM books FIRST 5;
SELECT TOP 5 FROM books;
SELECT * FROM books LIMIT 5;
GET 5 FROM books;

easy.prompt: Which query shows all distinct cities from customers?
answers: SELECT UNIQUE city FROM customers;
SELECT DISTINCT city FROM customers;
SELECT city DIFFERENT FROM customers;
SELECT * DISTINCT city FROM customers;

easy.prompt: How do you find products where price is greater than 100?
answers: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100;
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price >= 100;
SELECT * FROM products IF price > 100;
SELECT * FROM products FIND price > 100;

easy.prompt: Which query returns all columns from employees ordered by salary descending?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC;
SELECT * FROM employees SORT salary DESC;
SELECT * FROM employees DESC salary;
SELECT * FROM employees ORDER DESC BY salary;

easy.prompt: How do you select all students with a grade of 'A'?
answers: SELECT * FROM students WHERE grade = 'A';
SELECT * FROM students grade = A;
SELECT grade 'A' FROM students;
SELECT * FROM students IF grade = A;

easy.prompt: Which query counts all rows in the products table?
answers: SELECT COUNT FROM products;
COUNT(*) FROM products;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products;
SELECT COUNT(products);

medium.prompt: Which query groups orders by customer_id?
answers: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;
SELECT GROUP BY customer_id FROM orders;
SELECT customer_id FROM orders ORDER BY customer_id;
SELECT COUNT(customer_id) BY orders;

medium.prompt: Which INNER JOIN correctly connects orders to customers?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id;
SELECT * FROM orders JOIN customers WHERE customer_id = id;
SELECT * FROM orders, customers INNER customer_id = id;
SELECT * FROM orders MATCH customers ON id;

medium.prompt: Which query counts orders per city after a join?
answers: SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY city;
SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP city JOIN orders;
SELECT city, GROUP BY COUNT(*) FROM orders;
SELECT city FROM customers JOIN COUNT(orders);

medium.prompt: Which LEFT JOIN keeps all customers even without orders?
answers: SELECT * FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;
SELECT * FROM customers JOIN LEFT orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;
SELECT * FROM orders LEFT JOIN customers ON customers.id = orders.customer_id;
SELECT * FROM customers KEEP JOIN orders;

medium.prompt: Which query shows departments with more than 3 employees?
answers: SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM staff HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM staff GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;
SELECT department FROM staff WHERE COUNT(*) > 3;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) FROM staff ORDER BY department > 3;

medium.prompt: Which query joins employees with departments and shows their names?
answers: SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
SELECT employees.name, departments.name FROM employees JOIN departments;
SELECT * FROM employees, departments WHERE emp_id = dept_id;
SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e, departments d INNER e.dept_id;

medium.prompt: Which query finds the total sales by month?
answers: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM sales GROUP BY month;
SELECT month, SUM(amount) FROM sales GROUP BY month;
SELECT SUM(amount) FROM sales BY month;
SELECT month, SUM(amount) FROM sales;

medium.prompt: Which query uses JOIN to show customer names with their order totals?
answers: SELECT c.name, o.total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id;
SELECT name, total FROM customers, orders WHERE customer_id = id;
SELECT customers.name, orders.total FROM customers JOIN orders;
SELECT c.name, o.total FROM customers c, orders o;

medium.prompt: Which query groups sales by region and counts?
answers: SELECT region, COUNT(*) FROM sales GROUP region;
SELECT region, COUNT(*) FROM sales GROUP BY region;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sales BY region;
SELECT region FROM sales COUNT(*) GROUP BY region;

medium.prompt: Which RIGHT JOIN keeps all products even if not ordered?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders RIGHT JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.id;
SELECT * FROM products RIGHT JOIN orders ON products.id = orders.product_id;
SELECT * FROM products LEFT JOIN orders ON products.id = orders.product_id;
SELECT * FROM orders, products RIGHT JOIN ON product_id = id;

hard.prompt: Which query finds customers with no orders?
answers: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE orders IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM customers c WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id);
SELECT * FROM customers c WITHOUT orders;
SELECT * FROM customers EXCEPT orders;

hard.prompt: Which subquery returns products priced above the average price?
answers: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products);
SELECT AVG(price) FROM products WHERE price > products;
SELECT * FROM products ABOVE AVG(price);
SELECT * FROM products WHERE AVG(price) > price;

hard.prompt: Which query finds employees whose salary is the maximum in the table?
answers: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary = MAX(salary);
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary = (SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees);
SELECT MAX(salary) FROM employees WHERE employee = *;
SELECT * FROM employees HAVING salary = MAX(salary);

hard.prompt: Which query finds suppliers that do not appear in deliveries?
answers: SELECT * FROM suppliers s WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM deliveries d WHERE d.supplier_id = s.id);
SELECT * FROM suppliers WHERE supplier_id NOT deliveries;
SELECT * FROM suppliers EXCEPT SELECT * FROM deliveries;
SELECT * FROM suppliers WHERE deliveries IS EMPTY;

hard.prompt: Which correlated subquery keeps orders above that customer's average order total?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders o WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders);
SELECT * FROM orders o WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders i WHERE i.customer_id = o.customer_id);
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE AVG(total) < total GROUP BY customer_id;
SELECT * FROM orders o WHERE total > customer average;

hard.prompt: Which query shows students who scored above their class average?
answers: SELECT * FROM students WHERE score > AVG(score);
SELECT * FROM students s WHERE score > (SELECT AVG(score) FROM students);
SELECT * FROM students s WHERE score > (SELECT AVG(score) FROM students WHERE class = s.class);
SELECT AVG(score), * FROM students WHERE score > average;

hard.prompt: Which query finds products that have never been ordered?
answers: SELECT * FROM products WHERE NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM orders);
SELECT * FROM products p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.product_id = p.id);
SELECT * FROM products WHERE product_id NOT (SELECT product_id FROM orders);
SELECT * FROM products p WITHOUT (SELECT product_id FROM orders);

hard.prompt: Which subquery finds departments with total salary exceeding 100,000?
answers: SELECT dept_id FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id HAVING SUM(salary) > 100000;
SELECT * FROM departments WHERE (SELECT SUM(salary) FROM employees WHERE dept_id = departments.id) > 100000;
SELECT dept_id, SUM(salary) FROM employees WHERE SUM(salary) > 100000;
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE SUM(salary) > 100000 GROUP BY dept_id;

hard.prompt: Which query uses IN with a subquery to find orders from top customers?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT id FROM customers WHERE status = 'gold');
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = (SELECT id FROM customers WHERE status = 'gold');
SELECT * FROM orders IN customers WHERE customer_id = id AND status = 'gold';
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id IN SELECT FROM customers WHERE status = 'gold';

hard.prompt: Which correlated query finds the most recent order for each customer?
answers: SELECT * FROM orders o1 WHERE order_date = (SELECT MAX(order_date) FROM orders o2 WHERE o1.customer_id = o2.customer_id);
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date = MAX(order_date) GROUP BY customer_id;
SELECT * FROM orders o WHERE order_date = (SELECT MAX(order_date) FROM orders);
SELECT customer_id, MAX(order_date) FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;
